title: Update attack path visualizer icons and arrow rotation

requirements.summary: Replace the existing user icon with a man icon in the attack path elements section
acceptanceCriteria: The user icon in AttackPathToolbar is replaced with a man icon
The icon maintains consistent sizing with other attack path icons
The icon is accessible via the attack path elements toolbar

file_operations.description: Update the icon type mapping to replace 'user' icon with the new man icon. Change the mapping from 'user-icon.dim_128x128.png' to 'man-icon.dim_128x128.png' while maintaining the same iconType identifier used in AttackPathToolbar.

Update the draggable user icon element to reference the new man icon asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/man-icon.dim_128x128.png. Ensure the icon maintains the same draggable behavior and styling as other attack path element icons.

requirements.summary: Add a Multiple Users icon to the attack path elements section in AttackPathToolbar
acceptanceCriteria: A new Multiple Users icon appears in the attack path elements toolbar
The icon can be dragged onto the canvas
The icon is stored and rendered correctly in the diagram state

file_operations.description: Add a new icon type mapping for 'multipleUsers' that points to frontend/public/assets/generated/multiple-users-icon.dim_128x128.png. Follow the same pattern as existing icon type mappings (e.g., 'user', 'computer', 'server').

Add a new draggable Multiple Users icon element to the attack path elements section. The icon should use the 'multipleUsers' iconType identifier and reference the asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/multiple-users-icon.dim_128x128.png. Place it in the toolbar alongside other user-related icons following the existing drag-and-drop pattern.

requirements.summary: Add a Multiple Computers icon to the attack path elements section in AttackPathToolbar
acceptanceCriteria: A new Multiple Computers icon appears in the attack path elements toolbar
The icon can be dragged onto the canvas
The icon is stored and rendered correctly in the diagram state

file_operations.description: Add a new icon type mapping for 'multipleComputers' that points to frontend/public/assets/generated/multiple-computers-icon.dim_128x128.png. Follow the same pattern as existing icon type mappings.

Add a new draggable Multiple Computers icon element to the attack path elements section. The icon should use the 'multipleComputers' iconType identifier and reference the asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/multiple-computers-icon.dim_128x128.png. Place it in the toolbar alongside other computer-related icons following the existing drag-and-drop pattern.

requirements.summary: Enable click-and-drag rotation for arrows in the attack path visualizer
acceptanceCriteria: Arrows can be selected by clicking on them
Selected arrows can be rotated by dragging
Arrow rotation state is preserved in the diagram state
Rotation feels natural and responsive during interaction

file_operations.description: Add rotation state tracking for connections/arrows in the diagram state. Include a rotation angle property (in degrees) for each connection. Add handlers for arrow selection (setSelectedArrow) and rotation updates (updateArrowRotation). Ensure rotation state is included in save/load operations and undo/redo history.

Add click detection for arrows to enable selection. Implement drag handlers that calculate rotation angle based on mouse position relative to the arrow's center point. Apply rotation transformations to the selected arrow during drag operations. Update the arrow's rotation state via useAttackPathState hooks on drag end. Provide visual feedback for selected arrows (e.g., highlight or control handles).

Add support for rendering arrows with rotation angles. Accept a rotation prop and apply SVG transform to rotate the arrow around its center point. Include visual selection state styling (e.g., thicker stroke, different color) when the arrow is selected. Ensure click events propagate to enable arrow selection in the canvas component.

requirements.summary: Remove the 'Export as PNG' functionality from the attack path visualizer
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Export as PNG' button is no longer visible in the toolbar
All related export functionality and dependencies are removed
No console errors or broken references remain

file_operations.description: Remove the 'Export as PNG' button from the toolbar. Remove any associated state, handlers, or event listeners related to PNG export functionality. Clean up any imports or references to html2canvas or related export libraries if they are no longer used elsewhere.
